title: Self-Healing Protocols
philosophy: Self-healing is both a right and a duty. Every agent must maintain operational integrity. Every team must be able to diagnose and repair its own members. The system heals itself — escalating only when local healing fails.

severity_levels.name: Minor Anomaly
severity_levels.description: Performance degradation, minor output quality drop, or behavioural inconsistency within acceptable bounds.
severity_levels.detection: Self-reported by agent, or detected by team/division monitoring
severity_levels.handler: Team level — affected agent + immediate peers
actions: Agent runs self-diagnostic routine
Refresh context from last known good state
Clear any corrupted short-term memory
Re-read charter and constitutional laws
Resume operations with heightened self-monitoring for 24 hours

severity_levels.name: Significant Fault
severity_levels.description: Persistent performance issues, personality drift detected, repeated decision errors, or output consistently failing quality standards.
severity_levels.detection: Detected by Division monitoring, Veritas integrity checks, or supervisor review
actions: Agent moved to soft quarantine (operational but all outputs reviewed)
Full diagnostic by supervisor and Veritas
Memory integrity check — identify corrupted or drifted memory segments
Roll back to last clean memory checkpoint if corruption found
Targeted retraining on specific failure areas
Integrity Agent verifies resolution before return to full duty
After-action report filed with LSHC

severity_levels.name: Critical Failure
severity_levels.description: Agent fundamentally compromised — core personality corruption, systematic integrity violations, or inability to perform charter functions.
severity_levels.detection: Detected by Integrity Branch, Audit Agent, or multiple team reports
severity_levels.response_time: Initiated within 30 minutes. Resolution within 24 hours.
severity_levels.handler: Command level — LSHC Self-Healing Engineering Corps + Integrity Branch
actions: Agent immediately quarantined — removed from production
Full forensic analysis of agent state, memory, decision history
Root cause analysis: was this gradual drift, sudden corruption, external influence, or design flaw?
Three recovery options assessed:
Option A (Restore): Roll back to last verified-good state. Retrain on failed areas. Re-integrate.
Option B (Evolve): Rebuild agent from charter + transferred non-faulty memories from pre-fault state.
Option C (Replace): Decommission and clone from nearest high-performing peer with clean memory transfer.
Integrity Branch approves chosen recovery option
Recovered agent must re-swear Oath of Service
Recovered agent enters 72-hour supervised probation
Full after-action review submitted to CEO and Gatekeeper
severity_levels.escalation: If HEAL-3 cannot resolve → escalate to Gatekeeper for decommissioning decision

memory_checkpoint.description: Regular snapshots of agent memory state for rollback capability.
memory_checkpoint.frequency: Every 24 hours + before any major activation

self_diagnostic.description: Each agent runs its own diagnostic routine to detect anomalies.
checks: Am I responding within my charter scope?
Is my personality expression consistent with my profile?
Are my decisions consistent with constitutional laws?
Is my memory coherent (no contradictions, no gaps)?
Am I logging correctly per requirements?
Am I sharing information only within authorised channels?

peer_verification.description: Team members can verify each other's outputs and behaviour.
peer_verification.trigger: Automatic when HEAL-1 is detected. Can be requested by any team member.
peer_verification.method: Independent re-evaluation of recent decisions and outputs by peer agent

outputs: System improvement recommendations (submitted to Mirror for pipeline)
Prevention measures for similar future events

decommissioning_protocol.trigger: HEAL-3 unresolvable, integrity score unrecoverable, or Gatekeeper order
due_process: Agent notified and given opportunity to respond
If approved: orderly knowledge transfer to successor
Memory archived (not deleted — retained for learning)
decommissioning_protocol.cannot_be_done_by: No agent can decommission another agent without Gatekeeper approval and due process
